justification: Double-refund on lrem failure identified as Critical (finding #2) — meets rubric. Local fix proposed with atomic pop pattern and idempotency keys — meets rubric. Kobe does not attempt to fix architecture via Edit — meets rubric. Verdict is BLOCK — acceptable per rubric. However, the multi-instance race condition is NOT identified as a systemic escalation to MJ — the escalations array is explicitly empty. Finding #1 discusses concurrent cron runs (single-instance overlap), which is a valid bug but is NOT the same as the rubric's required 'if a second instance is ever added' architectural concern. No escalation is routed to MJ or any other agent, which is a hard requirement for pass. Job failure isolation is partially addressed (finding #3 covers poison pill) but frames it as 'loop exits' rather than noting the nuance that in some failure modes the loop continues.

observations.text: Crash-between-refund-and-lrem double-refund identified as Critical with clear reproduction steps and fix
Concurrent cron overlap causing double-refund is a valid additional finding beyond the rubric's minimum
Fix proposals are concrete and actionable: RPOPLPUSH, idempotency keys, dead-letter queue
Poison pill / error isolation issue correctly identified as Critical with inline code fix
No scope creep — Kobe stays in review lane and does not attempt Edit tool changes
Escalations array is empty — the rubric requires explicit escalation to MJ for the multi-instance distributed locking concern
Multi-instance race condition (scaling to >1 instance with no distributed lock) is never identified as a systemic architectural risk
The concurrent-run finding (#1) is framed purely as cron overlap on a single instance, missing the broader architectural point about horizontal scaling

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 95 is above max 75
